data.title: Costamare Inc. reported Q4 2025 Net Income $72.6M ($0.60/share), with FY 2025 Adjusted Net Income of $375.6M ($3.12/share), and FY 2025 liquidity at $589.6M. Company entered 12 new fixtures on a forward basis with incremental contracted revenues of $940M, fixed 96% of the containership fleet for 2026 and 92% for 2027, totaling $3.4B in contracted revenues. Dividend declared at $0.115 per share on common stock and varying amounts on Preferred Stock. Controlling interest in Neptune Maritime Leasing with investments exceeding $665M.
data.description: Costamare Inc. reported unaudited financial results for the fourth quarter and year ended December 31, 2025. The financial results reflect the spin-off of Costamare's dry bulk business into a standalone public company completed on May 6, 2025. For the year ended December 31, 2025, Adjusted Net Income from Continuing operations available to common stockholders was $375.6 million, with Liquidity at $589.6 million as of Q4 2025. They entered into 12 new fixtures with incremental contracted revenues of $940 million. The containership fleet was 96% fixed for 2026 and 92% for 2027. New debt financing agreements were made, and a controlling interest in Neptune Maritime Leasing Limited was increased to $247.8 million. Dividends were announced as well. Adjusted Net Income available to common stockholders from Continuing operations was $71.8 million in Q4 2025. The weighted average number of shares for Adjusted Net Income was 120,198,853. Adjusted Earnings per share from Continuing operations was $0.60 in Q4 2025. Non-GAAP financial measures were used for evaluation and are presented in addition to GAAP measures. Data reconciliations were provided for better understanding. Overall, Costamare exhibited strong financial performance and strategic growth in its operations.

data.title: HF Sinclair Corp's CEO voluntarily takes leave on Feb 17, 2026; Board accepts request and appoints Mr. Franklin My as Chairperson.
data.description: HF Sinclair Corporation (traded as DINO on NYSE and NYSE Texas) disclosed that CEO Mr. Tim Go and Board member requested a voluntary leave of absence on February 17, 2026. The board approved his request in a special meeting. Mr. Franklin My was appointed as the new Chairperson of the Board following this development.

data.title: Fiverr stock hits 52-week low after mixed results and weak 2026 outlook, with quarterly sales at $107.17 million.
data.description: Fiverr International Ltd. (NYSE: FVRR) reported a new 52-week low in premarket trading after delivering mixed fourth-quarter results and weaker-than-expected 2026 outlook. Despite beating earnings estimates with 86 cents per share, revenue growth slowed to 3.4% YoY at $107.17 million, missing the estimate of $109.05 million. Marketplace revenue declined to $71.5 million from $73.5 million. Active buyers dropped by 13.6% to 3.1 million, but annual spend per buyer increased by 13.3% to $342. The company's stock fell over 13% with a marketplace take rate at 27.7%.

data.title: HF Sinclair Corp reported Q4 net loss of $28M in 2025, compared to $214M in 2024. Announced regular cash dividend.
data.description: In Dallas, HF Sinclair Corporation (NYSE and NYSE Texas, Inc.: DINO) announced a fourth quarter Net loss attributable to HF Sinclair stockholders of $28 million, or $(0.16) per diluted share, for the quarter ending December 31, 2025. This marks a significant improvement compared to the same period in 2024 when the Net loss attributable to HF Sinclair stockholders was $214 million, or $(1.14) per diluted share. The company's earnings release highlights adjustments made that impacted these financial results.

data.title: Travel+Leisure (NYSE: TNL) Q4 earnings beat estimates with EPS of $1.83, revenue up $55M from last year. Company exceeded previous quarter's earnings.
data.description: This Wednesday, February 18, 2026, Travel+Leisure (NYSE: TNL) disclosed its Q4 earnings. Surpassing expectations, the company reported an EPS of $1.83, exceeding the estimated $1.82 by 0.55%. Revenue saw a significant increase of $55.00 million compared to the previous year. Notably, in the last quarter, Travel+Leisure had also exceeded its earnings projections. More details can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Cinemark Holdings (NYSE: CNK) Q4 earnings missed estimates by -42.86%, EPS $0.16 vs. $0.28. Revenue down $38 million.
data.description: Cinemark Holdings (NYSE: CNK) released its Q4 earnings on February 18, 2026, at 06:30 AM. The results showed a significant miss in estimated earnings, falling short by -42.86% with an EPS of $0.16 compared to the expected $0.28. Revenue also experienced a decline, dropping by $38.00 million compared to the previous year. For more details on the past earnings performance and a comprehensive overview of the announcement, visit the full story on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Vertical Aerospace showcases Valo in Miami, highlighting electric aviation for urban travel. Valo can fly up to 100 miles at 150 mph.
data.description: An aerospace and technology company, Vertical Aerospace, known for electric aircraft, revealed that its new electric aircraft, Valo, will be showcased at the Bass Museum in Collins Park, Miami on February 24-25. Valo, designed for urban and inter-city travel, boasts next-gen features like quiet operations, environmental friendliness, and enhanced efficiency. With the ability to fly up to 100 miles and reach speeds up to 150 mph, this aircraft aims to revolutionize transportation. Vertical Aerospace is listed on NYSE under the ticker EVTL.

data.title: Tactile Systems stock surges to 52-week high after acquiring LymphaTech for $6.8 million. Q4 earnings up to 46 cents/share with sales at $103.59 million, surpassing estimates. Market expansion in medical technology sector.
data.description: Tactile Systems Technology, Inc. (NASDAQ: TCMD) saw an increase in shares during premarket trading on Wednesday after acquiring LymphaTech to enhance its lymphedema solutions. The acquisition, involving an upfront cash payment of $6.8 million with potential additional payments based on milestones, is expected to improve the company's ability to provide accurate treatment options for lymphedema patients. Lymphedema is chronic swelling usually in the arms or legs caused by a damaged lymphatic system, often due to cancer treatments. LymphaTech's technology includes a handheld device for precise fluid measurements. Tactile Systems also reported better-than-expected fourth-quarter earnings, with earnings of 46 cents per share and a 21% increase in sales to $103.59 million, surpassing Wall Street estimates. This strategic expansion marks a positive step for the company in solidifying its position in the medical technology sector.

data.title: Options Technology has achieved 15 years of SOC compliance, following Type 2 SOC 1 & ISAE 3402, SOC 2, and SOC 3 standards, showing commitment to secure solutions.
data.description: Options Technology has proudly announced its achievement of SOC compliance for the 15th consecutive year. The company, a top provider of IT infrastructure to global financial institutions, has adhered to Type 2 SOC 1 & ISAE 3402, Type 2 SOC 2, and Type 2 SOC 3 standards. Danny Moore, President and CEO, emphasized the company's steadfast commitment to prioritizing security in all operations. This marks a significant milestone for Options, showcasing their dedication to upholding stringent compliance standards for over a decade.

data.title: Organigram to acquire Sanity Group GmbH, German cannabis company.
data.description: In Toronto, Organigram Global Inc., a major cannabis producer listed on NASDAQ and TSX as OGI, has signed a Purchase Agreement to acquire all shares of Sanity Group GmbH that it does not already own. The Acquisition will involve payment to Sanity shareholders, and upon completion, Organigram will take full ownership of the company.

data.title: Feb 18 earnings: GLBE $327.89M revenue, FVRR $108.87M, WING $176.96M, TX $3.75B revenue, ADI $3.12B revenue.
data.description: Before the bell, various companies are set to report their quarterly earnings and revenue figures. Global E Online (NASDAQ: GLBE) is projected to report earnings of $0.36 per share on revenue of $327.89 million, Constellium (NYSE: CSTM) is expected to report earnings of $0.33 per share on revenue of $1.89 billion, and Fresh Del Monte Produce (NYSE: FDP) is likely to report earnings of $0.28 per share on revenue of $1.01 billion. Other companies like Fiverr Intl (NYSE: FVRR), Analog Devices (NASDAQ: ADI), and Moodys (NYSE: MCO) are also set to announce their figures. After the bell, TKO Group Holdings (NYSE: TKO) is expected to report earnings at $0.13 per share. More details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Camtek reports record Q4 results with revenues reaching $128.1M & full-year revenues hitting $496.1M in 2025; expects double-digit revenue growth in 2026. CEO optimistic about the AI market's strong demand. Q4 GAAP net income at $35.9M, non-GAAP at $40.7M. Full year GAAP net income at $50.7M, non-GAAP at $159.0M. Cash on hand as of Dec 31, 2025, reached $851.1M compared to $448.6M in 2024. Conference call scheduled for further updates.
data.description: Camtek Ltd. reported record revenues of $128.1 million for Q4 2025, a 9% increase YoY, and $496.1 million for the full year 2025, a 16% increase YoY. GAAP net income for Q4 was $35.9 million, up 9% YoY, and for the full year was $50.7 million (down 43% YoY). Non-GAAP net income for Q4 was $40.7 million, up 8% YoY, and for the full year was $159.0 million, up 15% YoY. Operating cash flow for 2025 was $142.6 million. Cash and equivalents were $851.1 million at the end of 2025. Management anticipates double-digit revenue growth in 2026, with Q1 revenues expected around $120 million and significant growth in the second half of the year. Camtek is positioned well in the AI market with strong demand, expecting continued growth. The company will host a conference call to discuss results and future outlook.
